[React-admin - Theming](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/doc/3.19/Theming.html)<br/>4. [<ToggleThemeButton>](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/ToggleThemeButton.html)<br/>5. [Fix UserMenu Display Over Appbar by djhi · Pull Request #6105 · marmelab/react-admin](https://github.com/marmelab/react-admin/pull/6105)<br/><br/># React-admin AppBar Customization<br/><br/>## Default AppBar Elements<br/><br/>By default, the react-admin `<AppBar>` component displays several elements including a user menu button, loading indicator, theme toggle button, locale switcher, page title, and sidebar toggle.[1]<br/><br/>## Hiding the Theme Toggle<br/><br/>The theme toggle button appears in the AppBar automatically when you define a `darkTheme` prop on the `<Admin>` component.[2] To hide it, you can set the `darkTheme` prop to `null`:[2]<br/><br/>```javascript<br/>const App = () => (<br/>  <Admin darkTheme=\{null\}><br/>    // ...<br/>  </Admin><br/>);<br/>```<br/><br/>## Customizing the AppBar<br/><br/>Create a custom AppBar component based on react-admin's `<AppBar>` to modify its behavior:[1]<br/><br/>```javascript<br/>// in src/MyAppBar.js<br/>import \{ AppBar \} from 'react-admin';<br/><br/>const MyAppBar = () => <AppBar />;<br/>```<br/><br/>Then pass it to a custom layout:[1]<br/><br/>```javascript<br/>import \{ Layout \} from 'react-admin';<br/>import \{ MyAppBar \} from './MyAppBar';<br/><br/>export const MyLayout = (\{ children \}) => (<br/>  <Layout appBar=\{<MyAppBar />\}><br/>    \{children\}<br/>  </Layout><br/>);<br/>```<br/><br/>## UserMenu Customization<br/><br/>The AppBar accepts a `userMenu` prop to customize the dropdown user menu:[1]<br/><br/>```javascript<br/>const MyAppBar = () => (<br/>  <AppBar userMenu=\{<CustomUserMenu />\} /><br/>);<br/>```<br/><br/>## Key Props<br/><br/>The `<AppBar>` component supports `children`, `color`, `sx`, `toolbar`, and `userMenu` props for customization.[1] |

</details>

```json
{
  "url": "https://marmelab.com/react-admin/AppBar.html",
  "markdown": "React-admin - The AppBar Component\n\n# \n\nThe default react-admin layout renders a horizontal app bar at the top, which is rendered by the&#96; &#96; component.\n\nYour browser does not support the video tag.\n\nBy default, the&#96; &#96; component displays:\n\n- a button to display the user menu.\n- a loading indicator,\n- a button to change the theme (unless the [dark theme](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/Admin.html#darktheme) is disabled),\n- a button to change locales (if the application uses [i18n](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/Translation.html)),\n- the page title,\n- a hamburger icon to toggle the sidebar width,\n- [Index](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/Reference.html)\n\nYou can customize the App Bar by creating a custom component based on&#96; &#96;, with different props.\n\nTip: Don’t mix react-admin’s&#96; &#96; component with [Material UI’s component](https://mui.com/material-ui/api/app-bar/). The first one leverages the second but adds some react-admin-specific features.\n\n## Usage\n\nCreate a custom app bar based on react-admin’s&#96; &#96;:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n// in src/MyAppBar.js\nimport { AppBar } from 'react-admin';\nimport { Typography } from '@mui/material';\n\nconst MyAppBar = () =&gt;;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThen, create a custom layout based on react-admin’s&#96; &#96;:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n// in src/MyLayout.js\nimport { Layout } from 'react-admin';\nimport { MyAppBar } from './MyAppBar';\n\nexport const MyLayout = ({ children }) =&gt; (\n \n {children}\n \n);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThen pass this custom layout to the&#96; &#96; component:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n// in src/App.js\nimport { MyLayout } from './MyLayout';\n\nconst App = () =&gt; (\n \n // ...\n \n);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Props\n\n| Prop | Required | Type | Default | Description |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n| &#96;children&#96; | Optional | &#96;ReactElement&#96; | - | What to display in the central part of the app bar |\n| &#96;color&#96; | Optional | &#96;string&#96; | - | The background color of the app bar |\n| &#96;sx&#96; | Optional | &#96;SxProps&#96; | - | Style overrides, powered by MUI System |\n| &#96;toolbar&#96; | Optional | &#96;ReactElement&#96; | - | The content of the toolbar |\n| &#96;userMenu&#96; | Optional | &#96;ReactElement&#96; | - | The content of the dropdown user menu |\n\nAdditional props are passed to [the underlying Material UI element](https://mui.com/material-ui/api/app-bar/).\n\n## children\n\nThe&#96; &#96; component accepts a&#96;children&#96; prop, which is displayed in the central part of the app bar. This is useful to add buttons to the app bar, for instance, a settings button.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n// in src/MyAppBar.js\nimport { AppBar, TitlePortal } from 'react-admin';\nimport SettingsIcon from '@mui/icons-material/Settings';\nimport { IconButton } from '@mui/material';\n\nconst SettingsButton = () =&gt; (\n \n \n \n);\n\nexport const MyAppBar = () =&gt; (\n \n \n \n \n);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nTip: What’s the&#96; &#96;? It’s a placeholder for the page title, that components in the page can fill using [the component](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/Title.html).&#96; &#96; uses a [React Portal](https://react.dev/reference/react-dom/createPortal) under the hood.&#96; &#96; takes all the available space in the app bar, so it “pushes” the following children to the right.\n\nTip:&#96; &#96; renders a [Material-ui](https://mui.com/material-ui/react-typography/) element that you can customize by passing your own props:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nexport const MyAppBar = () =&gt; (\n \n \n \n \n);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nIf you omit&#96; &#96;,&#96; &#96; will no longer display the page title. This can be done on purpose, e.g. if you want to render something completely different in the AppBar, like a company logo and a search engine:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n// in src/MyAppBar.js\nimport { AppBar } from 'react-admin';\nimport { Box } from '@mui/material';\nimport { Search } from \"@react-admin/ra-search\";\n\nimport { Logo } from './Logo';\n\nconst MyAppBar = () =&gt; (\n \n \n \n \n \n \n);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## color\n\nReact-admin’s&#96; &#96; renders a Material UI&#96; &#96;, which supports a&#96;color&#96; prop to set the app bar color depending on the theme. By default, the app bar color is set to the&#96;secondary&#96; theme color.\n\nThis means you can set the app bar color to ‘default’, ‘inherit’, ‘primary’, ‘secondary’, ‘transparent’, or any string.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n// in src/MyAppBar.js\nimport { AppBar } from 'react-admin';\n\nexport const MyAppBar = () =&gt;;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## sx: CSS API\n\nPass an&#96;sx&#96; prop to customize the style of the main component and the underlying elements (see [the sx documentation](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/SX.html) for syntax and examples).\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n// in src/MyAppBar.js\nimport { AppBar } from 'react-admin';\n\nexport const MyAppBar = () =&gt; .RaAppBar-menuButton&#96; | Applied to the hamburger icon |\n| &#96;&amp; .RaAppBar-title&#96; | Applied to the title portal |\n\nTo override the style of&#96; &#96; using the [application-wide style overrides](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/AppTheme.html#theming-individual-components), use the&#96;RaAppBar&#96;   "markdown": "React-admin - ToggleThemeButton\n\n# \n\nThe&#96; &#96; component lets users switch from light to dark mode, and persists that choice by leveraging the [store](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/Store.html).\n\nYour browser does not support the video tag.\n\nIt is enabled by default in the&#96; &#96; as React-admin provides a [built-in dark theme](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/AppTheme.html#default).\n\n## Usage\n\nYou can add the&#96; &#96; to a custom [](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/AppBar.html#toolbar):\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n// in src/MyAppBar.js\nimport { AppBar, ToggleThemeButton } from 'react-admin';\n\nexport const MyAppBar = () =&gt; (\n} /&gt;\n);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThen, pass the custom App Bar in a custom&#96; &#96;, and the&#96; &#96; to your&#96; &#96;. The&#96; &#96; must define a&#96;darkTheme&#96; prop for the button to work:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nimport { Admin, Layout } from 'react-admin';\nimport { MyAppBar } from './MyAppBar';\n\nconst MyLayout = ({ children }) =&gt; (\n \n {children}\n \n);\n\nconst App = () =&gt; (\n \n ...\n \n);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Removing The Button From The AppBar\n\nThe&#96; &#96; appears by default in the&#96; &#96;. If you want to remove it, you have two solutions:\n\n- you can set the&#96; &#96;&#96;darkTheme&#96; prop to&#96;null&#96;:\n- [Index](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/Reference.html)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n// in src/App.tsx\nconst App = () =&gt; (\n \n // ...\n \n);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- or you can set a custom [ prop](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/AppBar.html#toolbar):\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n// in src/MyAppBar.tsx\nimport { AppBar, LocalesMenuButton, RefreshIconButton } from 'react-admin';\n\nexport const MyAppBar = () =&gt; (\n \n \n {/* no ToggleThemeButton here */}\n \n \n } /&gt;\n);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Creating A Dark Theme\n\nFor this button to work, you must provide a dark theme to the&#96; &#96; component. React-admin provides a [built-in dark theme](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/AppTheme.html#default), but you can override it according to your needs.\n\nThe&#96;darkTheme&#96; should be a JSON object that follows the [Material UI theme specification](https://material-ui.com/customization/theming/).\n\nYou can create such a theme from scratch:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nconst darkTheme = {\n palette: { mode: 'dark' },\n};\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nOf you can override react-admin’s default dark theme:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nimport { defaultDarkTheme } from 'react-admin';\n\nconst darkTheme = {\n ...defaultDarkTheme,\n palette: {\n ...defaultDarkTheme.palette,\n primary: {\n main: '#90caf9',\n },\n },\n};\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nTip: React-admin calls Material UI’s&#96;createTheme()&#96; on the&#96; &#96; prop - don’t call it yourself."

[Auth Providers](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/doc/3.19/Authentication.html)<br/>4. [React-admin - The AppBar Component](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/AppBar.html)<br/>5. [Search code, repositories, users, issues, pull requests...](https://github.com/marmelab/react-admin/issues/3033)<br/><br/># React-admin UserMenu Children and Custom Logout Content<br/><br/>To add custom content and a logout button to the UserMenu in react-admin, use the `useLogout()` hook.[1]<br/><br/>## Implementation<br/><br/>Create a custom logout button component using `useLogout()`:[1]<br/><br/>```javascript<br/>import \{ forwardRef \} from 'react';<br/>import \{ AppBar, Layout, UserMenu, useLogout \} from 'react-admin';<br/>import \{ MenuItem \} from '@mui/material';<br/>import ExitIcon from '@mui/icons-material/PowerSettingsNew';<br/><br/>const MyLogoutButton = forwardRef((props, ref) => \{<br/>  const logout = useLogout();<br/>  const handleClick = () => logout();<br/>  return (<br/>    <MenuItem ref=\{ref\} onClick=\{handleClick\}><br/>      <ExitIcon /> Logout<br/>    </MenuItem><br/>  );<br/>\});<br/>```<br/><br/>## Custom UserMenu with Children<br/><br/>Pass your custom content as children to the `UserMenu` component through the `AppBar`'s `userMenu` prop:[4]<br/><br/>```javascript<br/>const MyAppBar = () => (<br/>  <AppBar userMenu=\{<YourCustomUserMenu />\} /><br/>);<br/>```<br/><br/>The `UserMenu` accepts `children` which allows you to add custom menu items alongside the logout button.[2] Ensure the `\{logout\}` element is placed within the menu items themselves, not passed as a separate prop to `UserMenu`.[2]<br/><br/>## Key Points<br/><br/>- Use `useLogout()` hook to get the logout callback function[1]<br/>- Wrap custom logout buttons with `forwardRef` to allow Material UI keyboard navigation[1]<br/>- Place logout content inside MenuItem components within the UserMenu[2] |

</details>

[Auth Provider Setup](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/Authentication.html)<br/><br/># React-admin UserMenu Logout Default Children<br/><br/>React-admin's `UserMenu` component displays user-related actions in the top right corner of the admin layout, including logout functionality.[2]<br/><br/>## Creating a Custom Logout Button<br/><br/>To customize the logout behavior in a `UserMenu`, you can use the `useLogout()` hook to build a custom logout button.[1] The `useLogout()` hook returns a callback that logs the user out by calling `authProvider.logout()`.[1]<br/><br/>Here's a basic example:<br/><br/>```javascript<br/>import \{ useLogout \} from 'react-admin';<br/>import \{ MenuItem \} from '@mui/material';<br/>import ExitIcon from '@mui/icons-material/PowerSettingsNew';<br/>import \{ forwardRef \} from 'react';<br/><br/>const MyLogoutButton = forwardRef((props, ref) => \{<br/> const logout = useLogout();<br/> const handleClick = () => logout();<br/> return (<br/>  <MenuItem ref=\{ref\} onClick=\{handleClick\}><br/>   <ExitIcon /> Logout<br/>  </MenuItem><br/> );<br/>\});<br/>```<br/><br/>It's important to pass the `ref` to allow Material UI to manage keyboard navigation.[1]<br/><br/>## Prerequisites<br/><br/>The `UserMenu` only displays in applications using authentication, which requires setting up an `authProvider` with your `<Admin>` component.[2][5] |

</details>

Die Änderungen sind umgesetzt. Zusammenfassung:

## Änderungen in `AppBar.jsx`

### 1. **Ein Theme-Toggle**
- Eigener statischer Dark-Mode-Button entfernt
- Eigene Toolbar mit genau einem `ToggleThemeButton` von react-admin
- Keine doppelten Theme-Schalter mehr

### 2. **Rolle im User-Menü**
- Rolle wird im Dropdown über dem Logout angezeigt
- Name und Rolle stehen oben im Menü
- Rollen-Labels: Administrator, Account Executive, BDR, Partnership Manager, Marketing Manager

### 3. **Toolbar-Struktur**
- `LoadingIndicator` (Refresh)
- `ToggleThemeButton` (Theme wechseln)
- Notifications-Icon
- `CustomUserMenu` (Name, Rolle, Logout)

Das User-Menü zeigt jetzt:
- **Name** (aus `identity.name` oder `identity.fullName`)
- **Rolle** (z. B. Administrator, BDR)
- **Trennlinie**
- **Logout**

[React-admin - useLogout](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/useLogout.html)<br/><br/># Hiding the UserMenu in react-admin AppBar<br/><br/>To hide the default UserMenu in react-admin's AppBar without rewriting the entire AppBar component, you can pass `userMenu=\{null\}` to the AppBar.[1]<br/><br/>## Implementation<br/><br/>Create a custom AppBar component and pass `null` to the `userMenu` prop:<br/><br/>```jsx<br/>const MyAppBar = props => <AppBar \{...props\} userMenu=\{null\} />;<br/>```<br/><br/>This approach allows you to disable the user menu without having to completely rewrite the AppBar component.[1]<br/><br/>## AppBar Props<br/><br/>The `userMenu` prop is an optional prop of type `ReactElement` that controls the content of the dropdown user menu. By passing `null` instead of a component, you effectively hide it from display.[2]<br/><br/>Other AppBar props you can customize include `children`, `color`, `sx`, and `toolbar`.[2] |

{
  "url": "https://marmelab.com/react-admin/List.html",
  "markdown": "React-admin - The List Component\n\n# \n\nThe&#96; &#96; component is the root component for list pages. It fetches a list of records from the data provider, puts it in a [ListContext](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/useListContext.html), renders the default list page layout (title, buttons, filters, pagination), and renders its children. Usual children of&#96; &#96;, like [](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/DataTable.html), are responsible for displaying the list of records.\n\n## Usage\n\nHere is the minimal code necessary to display a list of posts using a [](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/DataTable.html):\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n// in src/posts.jsx\nimport { List, DataTable, DateField, BooleanField } from 'react-admin';\n\nexport const PostList = () =&gt; (\n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n);\n\n// in src/App.jsx\nimport { Admin, Resource } from 'react-admin';\nimport jsonServerProvider from 'ra-data-json-server';\n\nimport { PostList } from './posts';\n\nconst App = () =&gt; (\n \n \n \n);\n\nexport default App;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThat’s enough to display a basic post list, with functional sort and pagination.\n\nYou can find more advanced examples of&#96; &#96; usage in the [demos](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/Demos.html).\n\n## Props\n\n| Prop | Required | Type | Default | Description |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n| &#96;children&#96; | Optional * | &#96;ReactNode&#96; | - | The components rendering the list of records. |\n| &#96;render&#96; | Optional * | &#96;(listContext) =&gt; ReactNode&#96; | - | A function to render the list of records. Receive the list context as its argument |\n| &#96;actions&#96; | Optional | &#96;ReactElement&#96; | - | The actions to display in the toolbar. |\n| &#96;aside&#96; | Optional | &#96;ReactNode&#96; | - | The component to display on the side of the list. |\n| &#96;authLoading&#96; | Optional | &#96;ReactNode&#96; | - | The component to render while checking for authentication and permissions. |\n| &#96;component&#96; | Optional | &#96;Component&#96; | &#96;Card&#96; | The component to render as the root element. |\n| &#96;debounce&#96; | Optional | &#96;number&#96; | &#96;500&#96; | The debounce delay in milliseconds to apply when users change the sort or filter parameters. |\n| &#96;disable Authentication&#96; | Optional | &#96;boolean&#96; | &#96;false&#96; | Set to&#96;true&#96; to disable the authentication check. |\n| &#96;disable SyncWithLocation&#96; | Optional | &#96;boolean&#96; | &#96;false&#96; | Set to&#96;true&#96; to disable the synchronization of the list parameters with the URL. |\n| &#96;empty&#96; | Optional | &#96;ReactNode&#96; | - | The component to display when the list is empty. |\n| &#96;empty WhileLoading&#96; | Optional | &#96;boolean&#96; | &#96;false&#96; | Set to&#96;true&#96; to return&#96;null&#96; while the list is loading. |\n| &#96;error&#96; | Optional | &#96;ReactNode&#96; | - | The component to render when failing to load the list of records. |\n| &#96;exporter&#96; | Optional | &#96;function&#96; | - | The function to call to export the list. |\n| &#96;filters&#96; | Optional | &#96;ReactElement&#96; | - | The filters to display in the toolbar. |\n| &#96;filter&#96; | Optional | &#96;object&#96; | - | The permanent filter values. |\n| &#96;filter DefaultValues&#96; | Optional | &#96;object&#96; | - | The default filter values. |\n| &#96;loading&#96; | Optional | &#96;ReactNode&#96; | - | The component to render while loading the list of records. |\n| &#96;offline&#96; | Optional | &#96;ReactNode&#96; | &#96; &#96; | The component to render when there is no connectivity and there is no data in the cache |\n| &#96;pagination&#96; | Optional | &#96;ReactElement&#96; | &#96; &#96; | The pagination component to use. |\n| &#96;perPage&#96; | Optional | &#96;number&#96; | &#96;10&#96; | The number of records to fetch per page. |\n| &#96;queryOptions&#96; | Optional | &#96;object&#96; | - | The options to pass to the&#96;useQuery&#96; hook. |\n| &#96;resource&#96; | Optional | &#96;string&#96; | - | The resource name, e.g.&#96;posts&#96;. |\n| &#96;sort&#96; | Optional | &#96;object&#96; | - | The initial sort parameters. |\n| &#96;storeKey&#96; | Optional | &#96;string&#96;|&#96;false&#96; | - | The key to use to store the current filter &amp; sort. Pass&#96;false&#96; to disable store synchronization |\n| &#96;title&#96; | Optional | &#96;ReactNode&#96;/&#96;string&#96;/&#96;false&#96; | - | The title to display in the App Bar. |\n| &#96;sx&#96; | Optional | &#96;object&#96; | - | The CSS styles to apply to the component. |\n\n&#96;*&#96; You must provide either&#96;children&#96; or&#96;render&#96;.\n\nAdditional props are passed down to the root component (a MUI&#96; &#96; by default).\n\n## actions\n\nBy default, the&#96; &#96; view displays a toolbar on top of the list. It contains:\n\n- An&#96; &#96;\n- A&#96; &#96; if the resource has a creation view\n- A&#96; &#96; to display the filter form if you set the&#96;filters&#96; prop\n- [Index](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/Reference.html)\n\nThe&#96;actions&#96; prop allows you to replace the default toolbar by your own.\n\nFor instance, you can force the toolbar to display a Create button, even if the resource has no creation view, by passing a custom&#96; &#96; component:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nimport { List, ListActions } from 'react-admin';\n\nexport const PostList = () =&gt; (\n}&gt;\n ...\n \n);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou can also add custom actions, e.g. a [](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/SelectColumnsButton.html) to let the user choose which columns to display in the list:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nimport {\n CreateButton,\n DatagridConfigurable,\n ExportButton,\n FilterButton,\n List,\n SelectColumnsButton,\n TopToolbar,\n SearchInput,\n} from 'react-admin';\nimport IconEvent from '@mui/icons-material/Event';\n\nconst ListActions = () =&gt; (\n \n \n \n \n \n \n);\n\nconst postFilters = [\n,\n,\n];\n\nexport const PostList = () =&gt; (\n} filters={postFilters}&gt;\n \n ...\n \n \n);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nTip: If you are looking for an&#96; &#96;, check out this third-party package: [benwinding/react-admin-import-csv](https://github.com/benwinding/react-admin-import-csv).\n\nUse the&#96;useListContext&#96; hook to customize the actions depending on the list context, and the&#96;usePermissions&#96; to show/hide buttons depending on permissions. For example, you can hide the&#96; &#96; when the user doesn’t have the right permission, and disable the&#96; &#96; when the list is empty:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nimport {\n useListContext,\n usePermissions,\n TopToolbar,\n FilterButton,\n CreateButton,\n ExportButton,\n List\n} from 'react-admin';\nimport IconEvent from '@mui/icons-material/Event';\n\nconst ListActions = () =&gt; {\n const { total, isPending } = useListContext();\n const { permissions } = usePermissions();\n return (\n \n \n {permissions === \"admin\" &amp;&amp;}\n \n \n );\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## aside\n\nThe default&#96; &#96; layout lets you render the component of your choice on the side of the list.\n\nPass a React element as the&#96;aside&#96; prop for that purpose:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nconst Aside = () =&gt; (\n \n Post details \n \n Posts will only be published once an editor approves them\n \n \n);\n\nconst PostList = () =&gt; (\n}&gt;\n ...\n \n);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe&#96;aside&#96; component can call the&#96;useListContext()&#96; hook to receive the same props as the&#96; &#96; child component. This means you can display additional details of the current list in the aside component. For instance, you can display the total number of views of all posts in the list:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nimport { Typography } from '@mui/material';\nimport { useListContext } from 'react-admin';\n\nconst Aside = () =&gt; {\n const { data, isPending } = useListContext();\n if (isPending) return null;\n return (\n \n Posts stats \n \n Total views: {data.reduce((sum, post) =&gt; sum + post.views, 0)}\n \n \n );\n};\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe&#96;aside&#96; prop is also the preferred way to add a [Filter Sidebar](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/FilteringTutorial.html#the-filterlist-sidebar) to a list view:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n// in src/PostFilterSidebar.js\nimport { SavedQueriesList, FilterLiveSearch, FilterList, FilterListItem } from 'react-admin';\nimport { Card, CardContent } from '@mui/material';\nimport MailIcon from '@mui/icons-material/MailOutline';\nimport CategoryIcon from '@mui/icons-material/LocalOffer';\n\nexport const PostFilterSidebar = () =&gt; (\n \n \n \n \n}&gt;\n \n \n \n}&gt;\n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n// in src/PostList.js\nimport { PostFilterSidebar } from './PostFilterSidebar';\n\nexport const PostList = () =&gt; (\n}&gt;\n ...\n \n);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nTip: the&#96; &#96; prop in the&#96;PostFilterSidebar&#96; component above is here to put the sidebar on the left side of the screen, instead of the default right side.\n\n## authLoading\n\nBy default,&#96; &#96; renders the&#96; &#96; component while checking for authentication and permissions. You can display a custom component via the&#96;authLoading&#96; prop:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nimport { List } from 'react-admin';\n\nexport const PostList = () =&gt; (\n Checking for permissions...}&gt;\n ...\n \n);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## children\n\n&#96; &#96; itself doesn’t render the list of records. It delegates this task to its children components. These children components grab the&#96;data&#96; from the [ListContext](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/useListContext.html) and render them on screen.\n\nThe most common List child is [](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/DataTable.html):\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nexport const BookList = () =&gt; (\n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nReact-admin provides several components that can read and display a list of records from a&#96;ListContext&#96;, each with a different layout:\n\n- [](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/SingleFieldList.html) displays records inline, showing one field per record\n- [](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/Calendar.html) displays event records in a calendar\n- [](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/TreeWithDetails.html) displays records in a tree structure\n- [](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/SimpleList.html) displays records in a list without many details - suitable for mobile devices\n- [](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/EditableDatagrid.html) displays records in a table AND lets users edit them inline\n- [](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/DataTable.html) displays records in a table\n\nSo for instance, you can use a&#96; &#96; instead of a&#96; &#96; on mobile devices:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n// in src/posts.js\nimport * as React from 'react';\nimport { useMediaQuery } from '@mui/material';\nimport { List, SimpleList, DataTable, TextField, ReferenceField } from 'react-admin';\n\nexport const PostList = () =&gt; {\n const isSmall = useMediaQuery(theme =&gt; theme.breakpoints.down('sm'));\n return (\n \n {isSmall ? (\n record.title}\n secondaryText={record =&gt; &#96;${record.views} views&#96;}\n tertiaryText={record =&gt; new Date(record.published_at).toLocaleDateString()}\n /&gt;\n ) : (\n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n \n )}\n \n );\n};\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou can also render the list of records using a custom React component thanks to the&#96;render&#96; prop. Check [Building a custom List Iterator](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/ListTutorial.html#building-a-custom-iterator) for more details.\n\n## component\n\nBy default, the List view renders the main content area inside a Material UI&#96; &#96; element. The actual layout of the list depends on the child component you’re using (&#96; &#96;,&#96; &#96;, or a custom layout component).\n\nSome List layouts display each record in a&#96; &#96;, in which case the user ends up seeing a card inside a card, which is bad UI. To avoid that, you can override the main area container by passing a&#96;component&#96; prop:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n// use a div as root component\nconst PostList = () =&gt; (\n \n ...\n \n);\n\n// use a custom component as root component\nconst PostList = () =&gt; (\n \n ...\n \n);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe default value for the&#96;component&#96; prop is&#96;Card&#96;.\n\n## debounce\n\nBy default,&#96; &#96; does not refresh the data as soon as the user enters data in the filter form. Instead, it waits for half a second of user inactivity (via&#96;lodash.debounce&#96;) before calling the dataProvider on filter change. This is to prevent repeated (and useless) calls to the API.\n\nYou can customize the debounce duration in milliseconds - or disable it completely - by passing a&#96;debounce&#96; prop to the&#96; &#96; component:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n// wait 1 seconds instead of 500 milliseconds befoce calling the dataProvider\nconst PostList = () =&gt; (\n \n ...\n \n);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## disableAuthentication\n\nBy default, all pages using&#96; &#96; require the user to be authenticated - any anonymous access redirects the user to the login page.\n\nIf you want to allow anonymous access to a List page, set the&#96;disableAuthentication&#96; prop to&#96;true&#96;.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nimport { List } from 'react-admin';\n\nconst BoolkList = () =&gt; (\n \n ...\n \n);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## disableSyncWithLocation\n\nBy default, react-admin synchronizes the&#96; &#96; parameters (sort, pagination, filters) with the query string in the URL (using&#96;react-router&#96; location) and the [Store](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/Store.html).\n\nWhen you use a&#96; &#96; component anywhere else than as&#96; &#96;, you may want to disable this synchronization to keep the parameters in a local state, independent for each&#96; &#96; instance. This allows to have multiple lists on a single page. To do so, pass the&#96;disableSyncWithLocation&#96; prop. The drawback is that a hit on the “back” button doesn’t restore the previous list parameters.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nconst Dashboard = () =&gt; (\n \n // ...\n \n \n record.title}\n secondaryText={record =&gt; &#96;${record.views} views&#96;}\n tertiaryText={record =&gt; new Date(record.published_at).toLocaleDateString()}\n /&gt;\n \n \n \n \n record.title}\n secondaryText={record =&gt; &#96;${record.views} views&#96;}\n tertiaryText={record =&gt; new Date(record.published_at).toLocaleDateString()}\n /&gt;\n \n \n \n)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nTip:&#96;disableSyncWithLocation&#96; also disables the persistence of the list parameters in the Store by default. To enable the persistence of the list parameters in the Store, you can pass a custom&#96;storeKey&#96; prop.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nconst Dashboard = () =&gt; (\n \n // ...\n \n- \n+ \n record.title}\n secondaryText={record =&gt; &#96;${record.views} views&#96;}\n tertiaryText={record =&gt; new Date(record.published_at).toLocaleDateString()}\n /&gt;\n \n \n \n- \n+ \n record.title}\n secondaryText={record =&gt; &#96;${record.views} views&#96;}\n tertiaryText={record =&gt; new Date(record.published_at).toLocaleDateString()}\n /&gt;\n \n \n \n)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nPlease note that the selection state is not synced in the URL but in a global store using the resource and, if provided,&#96;storeKey&#96; as part of the key. Thus, all lists in the page using the same resource and&#96;storeKey&#96; will share the same synced selection state. This is a design choice because if row selection is not tied to a resource, then when a user deletes a record it may remain selected without any ability to unselect it. You can still opt out of all store interactions for list state if you set it to&#96;false&#96;.\n\n## empty\n\nWhen there is no result, and there is no active filter, and the resource has a create page, react-admin displays a special page inviting the user to create the first record.\n\nYou can use the&#96;empty&#96; prop to replace that page by a custom component:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nimport { Box, Button, Typography } from '@mui/material';\nimport { CreateButton, List } from 'react-admin';\n\nconst Empty = () =&gt; (\n \n \n No products available\n \n \n Create one or import from a file\n \n \n Import \n \n);\n\nconst ProductList = () =&gt; (\n}&gt;\n ...\n \n);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe&#96;empty&#96; component can call the&#96;useListContext()&#96; hook to receive the same props as the&#96;List&#96; child component.\n\nYou can also set the&#96;empty&#96; props value to&#96;false&#96; to bypass the empty page display and render an empty list instead.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nimport { List } from 'react-admin';\n\nconst ProductList = () =&gt; (\n \n ...\n \n);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## emptyWhileLoading\n\nDefault layout components (&#96; &#96; and&#96; &#96;) return null when the data is loading. If you use a custom layout component instead, you’ll have to handle the case where the&#96;data&#96; is not yet defined.\n\nThat means that the following will fail on load with a “ReferenceError: data is not defined” error:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nimport { List, useListContext } from 'react-admin';\nimport { Stack, Typography } from '@mui/icons-material/Star';\n\nconst SimpleBookList = () =&gt; {\n const { data } = useListContext();\n return (\n \n {data.map(book =&gt; (\n \n {book.title}, by {book.author} ({book.year})\n \n ))}\n \n );\n}\n\nconst BookList = () =&gt; (\n \n \n \n);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou can handle this case by getting the&#96;isPending&#96; variable from the [useListContext](https://marmelab.com/react-admin/useListContext.html) hook:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nconst SimpleBookList = () =&gt; {\n const { data, isPending } = useListContext();\n if (isPending) return null;\n return (\n \n {data.map(book =&gt; (\n \n {book.title}, by {book.author} ({book.year})\n \n ))}\n \n );\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe&#96; &#96; prop provides a convenient shortcut for that use case. That means that the export doesn’t take into account the selection and ordering of fields in your&#96; &#96; via&#96;Field&#96; components. If you want to customize the result, pass a custom&#96;exporter&#96; function to the&#96; &#96;. This function will receive the data from the&#96;dataProvider&#96;(after step 1) and replace steps 2-3 (i.e. it’s in charge of transforming, converting, and downloading the file).\n\nTip: For CSV conversion, you can import [jsonexport](https://github.com/kauegimenes/jsonexport#browser-import-examples), a JSON to CSV converter which is already a react-admin dependency. You’ll need to augment your objects based on relationships. For instance, the export for comments should include the title of the related post - but the export only exposes a&#96;post_id&#96; by default. For that purpose, the exporter receives a&#96;fetchRelatedRecords&#96; function as the second parameter. <think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Reviewing list actions**

I'm checking how custom actions replace default ones in the List component. I'm examining how the List component integrates with custom views like KanbanBoard and handles its own title bar rendering. 

**Checking title rendering**

The List component may render its own title from resource name, causing potential duplicate 'Deals' titles when wrapped in Box. The List component may add its own header with actions, potentially placing actions outside the main title area. 

The CreateButton with plus icon is in the List toolbar, not the header. I found that List view has CreateButton in ListActions while Kanban view has its own Add button in KanbanBoard. 

**Unifying create button**

I'm unifying the create button styles between List and Kanban views. I'm moving the Create button to the header and keeping filters in List view. 

**Implementing header layout**

I'm implementing the header layout with title, subtitle, and toolbar row containing view toggle, create button, filter, and refresh controls. I'm adding FilterButton to the header toolbar to show filters via popover. I'm moving CreateButton and FilterButton inside ListContext to ensure proper functionality. 

**Handling button contexts**

I'm adapting CreateButton and FilterButton to work outside of ListContext by leveraging react-admin's built-in context hooks. FilterButton in header needs access to ListContext, but header is outside List component structure. 

**Restructuring header placement**

I'm moving the header inside the List component to provide FilterButton access to ListContext. I'm implementing a unified toolbar with conditional actions using a custom layout. I'm hiding the List component's title and customizing its actions to include view toggle, create, filter, and refresh. I'm restructuring the header to include conditional toolbar elements: title, subtitle, and a toolbar with view toggle, create button, filter button (list view only), and refresh button. I'm extracting the toolbar elements into a TopToolbar component and passing them as actions to the List component. 

I'm adjusting the List component to hide its title and move toolbar actions to match the header layout. I'm adjusting the List component to move toolbar actions below the header in a separate Box. I'm moving toolbar actions into our header component to handle both kanban and list views consistently. I'm adjusting header placement to accommodate FilterButton's ListContext dependency by ensuring it's only rendered within List view. </details></think>

Die Änderungen sind umgesetzt. Zusammenfassung:

## Änderungen

### 1. **DealList.jsx**
- **Einheitliche Toolbar** direkt unter der Überschrift:
  - Ansicht-Umschalter (Liste / Kanban)
  - Create-Button „Deal erstellen“ (einheitliches Styling)
  - Filter-Button (nur in Listenansicht)
  - Refresh-Button
- **Layout**: Überschrift → Untertitel → Toolbar in einer Zeile
- **Listenansicht**: Header und Toolbar sind als Kind der List eingebunden, damit FilterButton im ListContext funktioniert
- **Kanban-Ansicht**: Toolbar ohne Filter-Button

### 2. **KanbanBoard.jsx**
- Neuer Prop `hideCreateButton`: Blendet den „Deal erstellen“-Button aus, wenn er im Header angezeigt wird
- `DealList` übergibt `hideCreateButton` an `KanbanBoard`

### 3. **Create-Button**
- Einheitliches Styling in beiden Ansichten: `variant="contained"`, gleiche Schatten und Hover-Effekte
- Ein Klick führt in beiden Ansichten zur Create-Seite (`/deals/create`)

Die Anpassungen sind umgesetzt. Zusammenfassung:

## Änderungen

### 1. **Toolbar unter der Überschrift (beide Ansichten)**
- Die Toolbar (Ansicht-Umschalter, Create, Refresh) steht jetzt in beiden Ansichten direkt unter der Überschrift „Deals“.

### 2. **Keine Toolbar mehr in der List**
- `actions={false}` bei der List, damit keine Buttons mehr im List-Container gerendert werden.

### 3. **Filter immer sichtbar**
- Pipeline- und Stage-Filter haben `alwaysOn`, damit sie ohne FilterButton angezeigt werden.
- Der FilterButton wurde entfernt, da er den ListContext benötigt und die Toolbar außerhalb der List liegt.

### 4. **DealsToolbar vereinfacht**
- Verwendet jetzt ein einfaches `Box` statt `TopToolbar`.
- Kein `FilterButton` mehr.

**Layout:**
- **Überschrift:** Deals  
- **Untertitel:** Verwalten Sie Ihre Sales Pipeline / Drag & Drop für Pipeline-Management  
- **Toolbar:** Ansicht-Umschalter | Deal erstellen | Refresh  
- **Listenansicht:** Filterzeile (Suche, Pipeline, Stage) → Tabelle  
- **Kanban-Ansicht:** Kanban-Board
